creative_brief: Hero image: founder crouching in a farm field with a young child, natural light, no stock-photo polish. Below the fold, candid photos from actual supplier farms (Bundarra, Collins, Paris Creek). Earthy palette — soil browns, leaf greens, warm whites. Typography is clean and personal, not corporate. The page should feel like a letter, not a pitch deck.
headline: I started this company the morning my daughter asked why her apple tasted like nothing
subhead: FarmThru exists because one parent got tired of pretending the big chains sold real food. Now we're building a grocery store that only stocks from farms we've visited ourselves.
hero_copy: She was four. She bit into it, made a face, and said "this doesn't taste like Grandma's ones." She was right. I'd been buying whatever was cheapest, from wherever. I couldn't name a single farm behind a single thing in my fridge. That morning I started calling farmers directly. What I learned changed everything — and became FarmThru.

sections.heading: What I found when I started asking questions
sections.body: Most of the produce in supermarkets passes through three or four middlemen before it reaches the shelf. Farmers get squeezed. Food sits in transit. Nutrients fade. Flavour disappears. I spoke with Rachel Ward at her regenerative farm in Kempsey, NSW. She told me the same thing every farmer told me: "We grow incredible food. The system between us and families is broken." So we built a different system. Zero warehouses. Zero wholesalers. Zero middlemen. FarmThru orders go from the farm to our Brookvale hub, and you collect when it's ready. That's it. No complicated supply chain eating margin and freshness along the way.

sections.heading: These are real farms, and I've stood on every one of them
sections.body: Bundarra Farm supplies our pork belly, pork and fennel salami, and little French ham. Collins Wild Caught brings in wild-caught Alaskan salmon and seasonal Australian seafood from the South Coast of NSW. Paris Creek Farms in Meadows, SA produces carbon-neutral biodynamic dairy. Farmer Brown supplies our eggs. Little Yarran Farm in NSW rounds out our growers. We're not a subscription box. We're an online grocery store stocked entirely by regenerative farms. Every product on our shelves is something we'd feed our own kids. That's not a slogan — it's our sourcing rule.

sections.heading: Why I'm asking you to help us grow
sections.body: We're preparing an equity crowdfunding campaign so everyday people — not just venture capitalists — can own a piece of this. I can't promise returns. I can't guarantee we'll get everything right. Building a new kind of grocery store is genuinely hard. But I can promise you this: every dollar of investment goes toward connecting more farms to more families across Sydney, starting from our Brookvale hub. If that matters to you, join the waitlist. A refundable $5 deposit holds your spot and tells us this model has legs. No lock-in. No obligation. Just a signal that you believe food should come from somewhere you can point to on a map.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: This is a pre-registration page for an upcoming equity crowdfunding campaign. No money is being raised at this stage. Any future offer will be made via a disclosure document on Birchal. This is not financial advice. Consider seeking independent financial advice before making any investment decision.
